task_observations: Hosted Access received an immediate ERR_INTERNET_DISCONNECTED failure for the DuskDS node request, displayed unavailable with Retry, marked Access Blocked and retained empty evidence arrays.
A separate hosted stalled request reached the product's visible five-second timeout state and still retained empty evidence arrays.
After restoring the Dusk node route, hosted Retry succeeded at 2026-07-21T00:31:57.938Z with block 3848665, one bounded 32-byte hash and exactly one automatic evidence entry.
The isolated public package started Safe mode on 5173 and 8788; a page-scoped Dusk node failure left the loopback Studio reachable while Access stayed Blocked with Retry and empty evidence.
After restoring the local page's Dusk node route, Retry succeeded at 2026-07-21T00:39:45.187Z with block 3848712, one bounded 32-byte hash and exactly one automatic evidence entry.

The package auto-opened and paired an existing default-Chrome tab, consuming the one-use bootstrap; the separately intended profile at the documented root received 401 health and 410 bootstrap responses and could not recover without restarting. This is CV-D-041 and is repaired in the local candidate with truthful status and exact --no-open recovery.

The live UI distinguished immediate unavailable from the five-second timeout, while persistent journey storage deliberately used the stable rpc-unavailable blocker for both and recorded no transient failure detail. This is assessed as an intentional evidence-minimization boundary rather than a defect.

traceability.promise: A developer can understand the hosted/local boundary and choose DuskDS or the DuskEVM pre-launch reference without duplicate path controls.
DuskDS prerequisites can be checked automatically or recorded manually with specific recovery, while DuskEVM remains pre-launch.
A developer can run or record a bounded read-only DuskDS latest-block observation without wallet or key material.
A developer can create or identify a DuskDS project, prove both WASM artifacts and record eligible VM-test evidence without unsafe project writes.
A developer can establish deployment readiness, complete a manual wallet handoff, and record bounded post-deploy read evidence without Studio signing.
Reviewed product resources, capabilities and network metadata are searchable, scoped, source-linked and recoverable from empty results.
A developer can find the relevant cause, bounded fix and recheck action for an active blocker or planned DuskEVM issue.
A developer can understand, install, pair, operate and stop Safe or Local Actions mode and see exact release parity.
A developer can inspect build identity, understand saved browser data, export safe diagnostics and reset the stated progress.
The default npm command starts paired loopback services without machine-action capabilities.
The explicitly named local-actions command enables only reviewed prerequisite and starter capabilities.
The direct CLI creates exactly one new reviewed DuskDS child project without overwrite or merge.
The package can verify startup, capabilities, preservation, shutdown and closed ports without a browser.
A one-use, short-lived, same-origin bootstrap establishes only an origin-bound HttpOnly local session.
The local static server exposes a bounded loopback health response without capability or secret disclosure.
Approved client routes fall back to the verified static Studio artifact.
Encoded traversal, undeclared assets and unsafe local static paths do not escape or reveal package contents.
Only the exact valid pairing flow creates a local companion session.
Authenticated health reports bounded capability and exact release identity.
Companion liveness is observable without leaking capabilities, environment values or local paths.
Authenticated Local Actions can run only the allowlisted bounded DuskDS prerequisite check.
Authenticated Local Actions can create only the reviewed DuskDS starter inside the managed root transactionally.
The dormant generic or DuskEVM template capability remains unavailable before reviewed activation.
Authentication is rejected before body parsing or capability work.
Wrong, expired, revoked and replayed sessions have no capability.
Only an exact allowlisted local Studio origin receives CORS or a session.
Only the exact configured loopback authority and port pass Host validation.
Private Network Access is denied unless separately reviewed and enabled.
Malformed, slow and oversized request bodies fail with bounded responses before work.
Pairing, per-session requests and concurrency are deterministically bounded.
Only cross-platform-safe names and paths contained by an approved root pass lexical validation.
Link and reparse components are rejected and containment is revalidated before promotion.
Existing content is never merged and concurrent creation has one atomic winner.
Injected population failures leave no final target and remove trusted temporary stages.
Tool failures, timeouts, output growth and tracked descendants remain bounded and cleaned up.
Diagnostics contain only allowlisted bounded fields and exclude paths and raw output.
Only exact reviewed command, argument, executable and request shapes can execute.
Stale, unreachable, incomplete or changed Dusk provenance fails the product build closed.
Generated, provider, local-state, credential and secret material is excluded from release scope.
Unexpected npm identity, contents, dependencies, scripts, integrity or provenance fail the package gate.
traceability.implementation: docs/security/security-test-matrix.md and npm package contract tests

traceability.promise: Safe mode cannot escalate, Local Actions stays allowlisted, projects survive and ports close.
The same package and security contract executes under normal users on Windows x64, Linux x64 and macOS arm64.
The package selected for publication is byte-for-byte the package that passed assurance.
Rust dependency, lock, advisory and informational-warning drift fail the reviewed gate.

defects.summary: Thirty of 44 reviewed troubleshooting records are unreachable because the UI allowlists only six DuskEVM and eight DuskDS records and has no all-scope control.
defects.root_cause: Troubleshooting was implemented only as a selected-path shortlist even though its source catalog and search promised reviewed recovery coverage.
defects.fix: Keep the focused common-issues scope but add an explicit all-reviewed-issues scope that exposes all 44 records and labels DuskEVM entries individually as pre-launch.
defects.regression_test: App: keeps common recovery focused while making every reviewed issue reachable

defects.summary: Reset all Studio progress clears persistent journey keys but not all session and module-memory state implied by the confirmation copy.
defects.root_cause: The confirmation headline overstated the saved scope, and older browser-history entries retained their builder-path state after reset so Back could restore and repersist the supposedly erased path.
defects.fix: Name the exact saved DuskDS journey scope, disclose session-only choices, and version builder-path history entries so reset invalidates every older entry in the tab before clearing the current path.
defects.regression_test: App requires inline confirmation and proves Reset followed by Back, Forward and remount never restores the cleared builder path.

defects.summary: The compatibility sslip.io hostname is regionally intercepted and fails trusted TLS in the current Romania environment; nip.io is the canonical production origin.
defects.source: live DNS, TLS and content observation
defects.qualification: The observation is real but does not describe the approved production surface. George explicitly selected the nip.io hostname as the production URL; public product guidance and validation use that canonical origin, whose current TLS and content checks pass. The sslip.io alias is therefore excluded from the release contract rather than treated as a product defect.
invalidation_evidence: Fresh canonical TLS and release-identity receipts pass; no campaign or user-facing instruction uses sslip.io as the production target

defects.summary: DuskEVM troubleshooting tells developers to retry an RPC health check and refresh after installing a wallet even though the same pre-launch surface exposes no RPC or wallet control.
defects.root_cause: Pre-launch safe-next-step copy had been updated, but the primary fix field retained instructions from a future live workflow.
defects.fix: Rewrite all six exposed DuskEVM recovery entries as explicit pre-launch planning and remove active wallet, funding, RPC, build and verification instructions.
defects.regression_test: Phase3App: keeps DuskEVM troubleshooting actions truthful during pre-launch

defects.summary: A DuskEVM Reference search can reveal a capability absent from the default scoped list, which can make the unfiltered scope look complete when it is only truncated.
defects.root_cause: The unfiltered selected-path capability list used a curated ID allowlist while search used the complete path-tagged catalog.
defects.fix: Build both filtered and unfiltered selected-path capability views from the same path tags and preserve the explicit disclosure limit.
defects.regression_test: App: shows the complete selected-path capability set before a search

defects.summary: Clean-start recovery exists in Build & browser data but is not discoverable from the path-selection surface when saved progress is already present.
defects.root_cause: The chooser indicated saved progress inside the DuskDS card but did not expose the settings/reset destination.
defects.fix: Show a saved-progress notice with a direct Review or reset saved progress action whenever DuskDS browser-local activity exists.
defects.regression_test: App: makes saved-progress review and clean-start recovery discoverable on the chooser

defects.summary: Hosted manual Setup can be saved complete without checking Node or npm even though the displayed new-starter Build command hard-requires Node >=24.18.0 <25 and fails later on an unsupported Node 24 release.
defects.root_cause: Setup correctly covered the Rust-native toolchain, but Build presented the separate npm template creator without a point-of-use Node boundary check.
defects.fix: Add a prominent Node/npm prerequisite immediately before Prepare project and repeat the exact engine guard before any directory creation or package download.
defects.regression_test: Phase2App: uses the exact npm template creator and pins its reviewed WSL test to Rust 1.94.0

defects.summary: Troubleshooting search for WSL does not recognize WSL_E_DISTRO_NOT_FOUND or provide the direct reviewed Ubuntu 24.04 installation and recheck recovery.
defects.root_cause: The Linux-required recovery entry omitted exact Windows WSL failure terms and exposed only a generic return to Build.
defects.fix: Add exact missing-distribution search terms, Ubuntu 24.04 install/recheck guidance, a copyable install command and a direct return to Setup.
defects.regression_test: Phase3App: recognizes the exact missing Ubuntu WSL failure and routes back to Setup

defects.summary: Activating Continue manually from the dynamic Setup or Build Local Studio fallback removes the focused control and leaves keyboard focus on the document body.
defects.root_cause: The state transition unmounted the invoking button without explicitly focusing the newly rendered manual lane.
defects.fix: Focus the new manual-lane heading after the dynamic panel transition.
defects.regression_test: Phase3App: moves focus into the manual lane when a dynamic Local Studio panel is removed

defects.summary: The DuskEVM RPC copy was reported as lacking confirmation, but the shared CopyButton exposes a visible Copied state and an atomic polite status; another black-box execution observed the exact confirmation.
invalidation_evidence: App.test: Copy pre-launch RPC URL copied to clipboard

defects.summary: Ordinary Reference query changes can recover visible results without announcing the updated document, capability and network counts.
defects.root_cause: The live resultAnnouncement was populated only by explicit clear and broaden actions, not ordinary query-result changes.
defects.fix: Debounce and announce result counts for ordinary nonempty query changes.
defects.regression_test: Phase3App: announces ordinary Reference search recovery counts

defects.summary: Keyboard traversal through Inspect is operable but requires 28 tab stops to readiness and a further 26 to footer settings, suggesting a missing long-page bypass or landmark aid.
defects.root_cause: Inspect exposed four long independent work sections without direct within-page section navigation.
defects.fix: Add an Inspect section navigator that scrolls and focuses Latest block, Source identity, Deployment readiness and Data driver.
defects.regression_test: Phase3App: provides direct focusable navigation across the long Inspect page

defects.summary: Access evidence can complete out of sequence and unlock Build while required Setup remains incomplete.
defects.root_cause: Journey normalization treated complete evidence as sufficient for a passed step before evaluating whether the previous required step was complete.
defects.fix: Retain legitimate out-of-order evidence but keep the step and all downstream steps uncompleted until the previous step is complete or explicitly skipped; keep later pages reviewable while disabling their evidence-producing actions until the preceding step has that truthful disposition.
defects.regression_test: journeyProgress retains evidence without unlocking a gated step; Phase2App disables Access, Build and Inspect evidence actions until their preceding steps are complete or skipped

defects.summary: A pathless Troubleshooting recovery action changes the hash to Build but renders Overview because no builder path is selected.
defects.root_cause: Recovery actions were plain hash links and could not establish the DuskDS path required by guide routes.
defects.fix: Make recovery actions route-aware buttons that select DuskDS when needed before opening Setup or Build.
defects.regression_test: App: selects DuskDS before opening a recovery action from a pathless session

defects.summary: Unknown hashes and DuskEVM-incompatible step hashes can remain in the address bar while a different surface is rendered.
defects.root_cause: Route fallback and DuskEVM canonicalization changed the rendered route without replacing the noncanonical hash.
defects.fix: Canonicalize unknown hashes through useRoute and replace DuskEVM Access, Build and Inspect hashes with the pre-launch Setup route.
defects.regression_test: Phase3App: returns from DuskEVM support routes to the pre-launch overview

defects.summary: Invalid manual Access values report a generic alert but do not identify, describe or focus the invalid field.
defects.root_cause: Manual validation stored only one page-level message and did not bind error state to the failing height or hash input.
defects.fix: Track the failing field, expose aria-invalid and aria-describedby, clear stale errors on edit and focus the first invalid input.
defects.regression_test: Phase2App: identifies and focuses the invalid manual Access field

defects.summary: The unsupported-Node CLI refusal gives the exact range but no direct official download, version check or rerun guidance.
defects.root_cause: The engine guard emitted only the required semver range and relied on external documentation for recovery.
defects.fix: Add the official Node 24.18.0 archive link, new-terminal instruction, node --version confirmation and explicit rerun step to the refusal.
defects.regression_test: scripts/test-npm-package.mjs asserts exact unsupported-Node recovery guidance

defects.summary: A preliminary non-browser GET correlated with a later fresh browser remaining unpaired until the package was restarted browser-first.
defects.qualification: The isolated sequence was correlational; the local runtime changes bootstrap state only for the guarded POST endpoint.
invalidation_evidence: staticServer.test: serves preliminary GET and HEAD requests without consuming the one-time same-origin bootstrap
packages/local-runtime/src/staticServer.ts: only POST /__dusk/bootstrap enters the bootstrap state machine

defects.summary: Expanding a long Reference list moves the same collapse control thousands of pixels below the viewport.
defects.root_cause: Each disclosure toggle was rendered after the variable-length list, so expansion changed the control's document position.
defects.fix: Place the stable toggle before its list so the control remains in view and focus position when the list expands.
defects.regression_test: Playwright: mobile long-page navigation and Reference disclosure controls stay reachable

defects.summary: Global navigation scrolls away on very long pages at 760 CSS pixels and below.
defects.root_cause: The mobile breakpoint made the entire Studio header static to avoid a tall stacked sticky header.
defects.fix: Use a compact sticky four-control mobile navigation, hide the redundant brand row and preserve the contextual return row only when needed.
defects.regression_test: Playwright: mobile long-page navigation and Reference disclosure controls stay reachable

defects.summary: Troubleshooting visually concatenates the Recheck label and its instruction.
defects.root_cause: The inline strong and small elements had no grouping, punctuation, gap or block layout.
defects.fix: Group the label and instruction in a spaced block and punctuate the label explicitly.
defects.regression_test: App and Playwright assert the visible Recheck: label

defects.summary: Manual DuskDS Setup requires opening six separate disclosures to collect the required check commands.
defects.root_cause: The manual checklist optimized each tool for individual diagnosis but exposed no aggregate execution path for the common first-run case.
defects.fix: Add one fail-closed platform-specific bundle that copies every required Setup check while preserving the individual expected results, repairs and official help links.
defects.regression_test: manualJourneyConfig and Phase2App assert the aggregate required-check bundle and per-tool recovery rows

defects.summary: The path chooser's accessible group label is ignored because aria-label is placed on a generic div without a group role.
defects.root_cause: The path-card container had a group label but no role that permits the label or exposes the cards as one named choice set.
defects.fix: Expose the path-card container as a role=group named Choose a builder path while retaining the two native button controls.
defects.regression_test: App: renders a named Choose a builder path group

defects.summary: Reference disclosure buttons change their text but do not expose expanded state or identify the controlled result list.
defects.root_cause: The custom disclosure buttons were implemented as visual toggles without aria-expanded or aria-controls.
defects.fix: Bind both documentation and capability controls to stable result-list ids and expose their current aria-expanded state.
defects.regression_test: Phase3App: asserts Reference disclosure expanded state and controlled result ids

defects.summary: The one-use local bootstrap accepted Host 127.0.0.1 with Origin localhost and issued a session cookie despite the exact same-origin boundary.
defects.root_cause: The local static server independently allowlisted both loopback spellings instead of requiring the Origin spelling to match the accepted Host header.
defects.fix: Derive the accepted bootstrap origin from the validated Host header and require the exact same loopback representation and port.
defects.regression_test: staticServer.test rejects both localhost-to-127.0.0.1 mismatch directions before accepting an exact localhost pair

defects.summary: The documented Ctrl+C shutdown did not stop the public package under automated Windows control-event delivery, while CTRL_BREAK_EVENT did close both listeners.
defects.root_cause: On Windows, npx.cmd may intercept Ctrl+C with a Terminate batch job (Y/N)? confirmation. The runtime remained contained and foreground-only, but the public shutdown guidance omitted the required Y and Enter response.
defects.fix: Keep the standard Ctrl+C instruction and add a Windows-specific Y-and-Enter step everywhere the local Studio shutdown is documented or rendered.
defects.regression_test: main.test asserts the Windows-specific runtime banner while README, packaged README and Local Studio recovery copy carry the same instruction.
defects.verification_receipt: A campaign-owner interactive PTY reproduction of the exact public package showed the npx confirmation; entering Y and pressing Enter exited immediately and closed both 5173 and 8788.

defects.summary: The packaged and repository README files are not byte-identical.
defects.qualification: The two artifacts serve different user contexts, make no byte-identity promise and agree on the package version, runtime, installation and safety contract.
invalidation_evidence: CV-X-NPM-02-001 task observation that release, runtime and safety instructions agree

defects.summary: The provenance-bound v1.0.1 tag has no separate formal GitHub Release object.
defects.qualification: The public package contract promises npm distribution, a public tag, source commit and provenance; it does not promise a separate GitHub Releases surface.
invalidation_evidence: CV-X-NPM-02-001:E2 exact tag, commit, workflow and provenance binding
Public npm package metadata and repository tag v1.0.1

defects.summary: The DuskEVM pre-launch RPC endpoint could be copied but was not visible for inspection.
defects.root_cause: The endpoint lived only in the CopyButton value while the nearby network context showed the name and chain identifiers.
defects.fix: Render the exact endpoint in a labelled command surface beside its copy control and state that it is an inspectable pre-launch reference, not a live Studio connection.
defects.regression_test: App and Phase2App assert that the exact RPC endpoint is visibly rendered on the DuskEVM pre-launch page.

defects.summary: A fresh Codex browser could not reach critical first-party Dusk documentation through Cloudflare and the Studio exposed no alternate authoritative source route.
defects.root_cause: The pre-launch page and general Reference surface relied on rendered Dusk documentation even though fresh automated browsers can encounter Cloudflare and the official documentation source repository is public and authoritative.
defects.fix: Add a clearly named Official docs source link to the dusk-network/docs repository alongside the DuskEVM deployment guide and as a global Reference fallback.
defects.regression_test: App, Phase2App, Phase3App and Playwright assert the official documentation source link and exact repository URL.

defects.summary: The identifier helper accepted noncanonical or unbounded hexadecimal quantities and did not explain why long hex values can legitimately classify as blocks rather than malformed addresses or hashes.
defects.root_cause: Block recognition used an unbounded hex-or-decimal regex and the helper text mentioned only a short hexadecimal example.
defects.fix: Accept only canonical unsigned JSON-RPC quantities bounded to 256 bits, preserve exact address and transaction precedence, and explain the long-value ambiguity.
defects.regression_test: evmRead rejects leading-zero and over-256-bit quantities while App accessibility copy explains canonical quantity and identifier-length precedence.

defects.summary: Selecting Existing repository immediately displayed repeated empty-field validation alerts before the developer interacted with the project-root field.
defects.root_cause: The existing-root path validator ran as soon as the project mode changed, and three downstream command sections repeated the same error as live alerts whenever commands were unavailable.
defects.fix: Delay the field-level alert until the root is changed or blurred, connect it with aria-invalid and aria-describedby, and use non-alert command-context prompts in downstream sections.
defects.regression_test: App verifies no existing-root error appears immediately after project-mode selection, then verifies one linked accessible error after field interaction; all 30 App tests and type checking pass.

defects.summary: Clearing a zero-result Troubleshooting search removes the focused button and leaves keyboard focus on the document body.
defects.root_cause: Troubleshooting cleared query state inline without retaining a search-input reference or deliberately restoring focus after the conditional Clear search control unmounted.
defects.fix: Route empty-search recovery through a dedicated clearSearch action that clears the query and focuses the Troubleshooting search input on the next animation frame.
defects.regression_test: Phase3App and the five-project Playwright matrix assert that clearing a zero-result Troubleshooting query restores the full list and focuses the empty search input.

defects.summary: The empty Troubleshooting state tells developers to open project support but provides no contextual support link.
defects.root_cause: The empty-state copy referenced support while the only support link remained in the distant global footer.
defects.fix: Render an Open project support link beside Clear search in the empty Troubleshooting recovery actions.
defects.regression_test: Phase3App and Playwright assert the contextual support link and exact public issues URL in the zero-result state.

defects.summary: Browser Back can reopen a DuskEVM history entry as DuskDS Setup because both paths share the same route hash and rendering follows the latest global path selection.
defects.root_cause: Builder path lived only in mutable React and localStorage state while history entries stored only hashes such as #setup, so entries for different paths were indistinguishable.
defects.fix: Persist the selected builder path into each history entry, copy it into new internal navigation entries and restore it on popstate or hash navigation while retaining browser-local persistence for reloads.
defects.regression_test: The five-project Playwright matrix creates EVM Setup, Overview and DuskDS Setup entries, then proves two Back operations restore Overview followed by the original EVM entry.

defects.summary: Browser Back to the long path-selection page loses the entry's prior scroll position and returns to the top.
defects.root_cause: The route layout effect unconditionally scrolled every render to zero and history entries did not retain a route-specific scroll position.
defects.fix: Use manual history scroll restoration, persist bounded scroll positions per entry, restore the active entry after rendering and update the document title in the same layout phase.
defects.regression_test: The five-project Playwright matrix records a greater-than-500-pixel overview position, opens DuskDS, goes Back and requires the exact prior scroll position and overview title.

defects.summary: Seven DuskDS Setup tool-help links were reported as unnamed and focusable.
defects.qualification: The pilot's DOM sweep used innerText on links inside closed details elements, where hidden content correctly reports an empty rendered string. The links are not exposed while their disclosure is closed and have text plus the new-tab announcement when revealed. Labels were nevertheless made tool-specific to improve link-list clarity.
invalidation_evidence: ManualToolRow renders each help link inside its native details disclosure with visible text children
Phase2App reveals the Git disclosure and resolves a link named Git installation and help with the exact reviewed URL

defects.summary: WebKit reported repeated blocked inline-stylesheet CSP messages during the screenshot-heavy pilot.
defects.qualification: The application contains no inline style element or JSX style attribute, and a separate fresh WebKit production probe traversed all nine routes with reduced motion and no screenshots while recording zero console errors. The messages align with Playwright's screenshot-time CSS injection being correctly blocked by the production CSP.
invalidation_evidence: CV-E-OPERATOR-WEBKIT-CSP-20260720 all-route no-screenshot probe: zero console errors
Source search found no inline style element, style attribute or stylesheet injection in the Studio application

defects.summary: Build and Inspect validation alerts were not programmatically associated with the invalid evidence field and did not move focus to the field requiring recovery.
defects.root_cause: Form-level alert state retained only an error string, so the UI could not identify the invalid field, bind it with aria-invalid and aria-describedby, or focus it after a failed save.
defects.fix: Return precise field identities from bounded evidence validators, bind each active alert to its field or confirmation, focus it after validation failure, cover source mismatches as well as format failures, and clear only the affected state when the developer edits it.
defects.regression_test: manualEvidenceValidation records exact invalid-field identities; Phase2App identifies, describes and focuses invalid Access, Build artifact/source, Inspect source, contract ID, response digest, function name and confirmation controls.

defects.summary: A fixed-port startup conflict failed closed and cleaned partial services but exposed only raw EADDRINUSE output without an actionable recovery path.
defects.root_cause: The runtime rethrew the Node listen error after cleanup and the public CLI printed its raw message without classifying the fixed-port conflict.
defects.fix: Track which fixed loopback port is starting, translate EADDRINUSE into a product-level explanation with release-and-rerun guidance, and explicitly confirm that partial Studio services were stopped.
defects.regression_test: local-runtime main tests require actionable, cleanup-aware port-conflict guidance without exposing raw EADDRINUSE; exact candidate conflict smoke and final-candidate pilot retest remain required.

defects.summary: When automatic launch paired a different default-browser profile, the intended profile showed a misleading paired-ready companion page and had no discoverable path to the existing --no-open recovery.
defects.root_cause: The one-use bootstrap correctly burned after the first profile paired, but the Local Companion page rendered a paired-ready heading and Safe-mode instructions for every npm runtime state, while public run documentation did not explain how to choose a browser profile with --no-open.
defects.fix: Render connection-state-specific page titles and content, show a dedicated not-paired recovery lane with exact Safe and Local Actions --no-open commands, explain the one-profile/five-minute boundary in runtime output, and add the same recovery to public package documentation without weakening the one-use bootstrap.
defects.regression_test: App verifies an expired or already-consumed bootstrap renders the truthful not-paired heading, exact recovery explanation and both version-pinned --no-open commands; local-runtime tests verify automatic and manual-profile pairing instructions.

defects.summary: Immediate DuskDS node unavailability and a visible five-second timeout persist the same rpc-unavailable journey blocker code.
defects.qualification: The live request state and visible notice distinguish unavailable from timeout while the durable journey record intentionally stores only the stable recovery category and never transient error text. This minimizes persisted diagnostics and still preserves the truth required for gating: Access is blocked and no evidence was recorded.
invalidation_evidence: CV-X-RES-03-002:E2 immediate unavailable UI plus blocked empty-evidence storage
CV-X-RES-03-002:E3 five-second timeout UI plus the same blocked empty-evidence storage
CV-X-RES-03-002:E4 and E6 successful retry records exactly one bounded receipt only after recovery

defects.summary: The final campaign gate accepted a fabricated but structurally polished 32-pilot completion record without preserved receipt bytes, scenario-specific clean-state proof, a complete defect ledger or required completion fields.
defects.root_cause: The validator trusted self-asserted arrays and flags, treated every black-box attempt as counted, did not resolve references or hash receipt files, and iterated only completion and defect fields supplied by the evidence itself.
defects.fix: Make counted outcome-based, bind every pass to its scenario context and clean-state profile, anchor evidence references, require hash-verified repository receipts for final pilots/retests/challenges/package assurance, preserve an exact policy defect ledger, require named completion flags and bind the policy bytes used by the final gate.
defects.regression_test: test-comprehensive-validation builds a valid receipt-bound synthetic final and rejects outcome-count, context, clean-state, reference, receipt, defect-ledger, completion and challenge forgeries.

defects.summary: Existing-repository evidence could record HEAD while tracked or untracked files differed from that commit, and abbreviated seven-character identities were accepted.
defects.root_cause: The existing-project command initially ran only git rev-parse HEAD and the shared revision validator accepted abbreviated identities. The first repair checked cleanliness only before later build, VM-test and hashing actions, so source could still change afterward while the stale clean HEAD was accepted.
defects.fix: Require a full 40- or 64-character verified object identity, an initial tracked/untracked clean-tree check, and a final post-build check that the same full HEAD remains checked out and the tracked plus unignored working tree remains clean. Store the bounded source scope and post-build revalidation claim; explicitly exclude ignored local files from the source claim and prohibit treating them as build inputs.
defects.regression_test: manualEvidenceValidation and journeyProgress reject abbreviated identities; Phase2App verifies Windows/POSIX initial and post-build commands, same-commit enforcement, explicit final confirmation, bounded source metadata and storage.

defects.summary: All reviewed troubleshooting could label less-common DuskEVM records as active recovery, and a DuskDS Open Build action kept an already-selected DuskEVM path and landed on the wrong surface.
defects.root_cause: Pre-launch classification reused the six-item common shortlist, and recovery actions selected DuskDS only when no path existed.
defects.fix: Maintain an explicit complete DuskEVM pre-launch issue classification and deliberately switch every DuskDS route action to DuskDS before navigation.
defects.regression_test: App verifies a non-shortlisted Blockscout record is Planning with Review before launch, and an Open Build action from an EVM-selected session renders DuskDS Build.

defects.summary: Canonicalizing a DuskEVM-only direct hash such as #build pushed #setup, so browser Back returned to #build and recreated the same redirect trap.
defects.root_cause: Canonical route repair used the ordinary push-navigation API instead of replacing the invalid history entry.
defects.fix: Add explicit replace navigation and use it for DuskEVM guide-route canonicalization.
defects.regression_test: Phase3App proves direct #build canonicalizes to #setup, Back reaches the prior Overview entry and Forward returns to #setup without reopening #build.

defects.summary: Public product and design documents promised a four-step selected-path guide for both paths while the truthful pre-launch DuskEVM UI intentionally provides one learning surface.
defects.root_cause: The interaction model changed to a single DuskEVM pre-launch surface but the higher-level product contract retained its earlier shared-journey wording.
defects.fix: Formally define Setup to Inspect as the DuskDS evidence journey and explicitly exempt pre-launch DuskEVM from four live stages and completion scoring until activation.
defects.regression_test: README, PRODUCT and DESIGN now state the same pre-launch exception; existing App and Phase3App tests enforce the one-surface, no-score UI.

defects.summary: Final traceability accepted generic evidence references without proving the claimed product surface or distinguishing success, failure/recovery and final-candidate outcomes.
defects.root_cause: The validator resolved evidence references only to a known receipt-bound record and did not bind each reference to a canonical surface and one typed outcome.
defects.fix: Require receipt trace_claims with exact reference, canonical surface_id and typed success, failure-recovery or final-candidate outcome; reject duplicate global use so one reference proves only one surface and outcome.
defects.regression_test: The valid fixture supplies distinct typed claims for every required surface and outcome; adversarial one-reference-for-all, wrong-outcome and duplicate-reference fixtures are rejected.

defects.summary: Final source, automation, package, registry and production readiness could be asserted without inspecting the current clean repository, computing package bytes or binding immutable external observations.
defects.root_cause: The final gate compared evidence fields to one another but did not derive current HEAD, tag and tarball digests or require typed receipt-bound automation, platform, registry-provenance and production-manifest results.
defects.fix: Derive clean HEAD, exact local tag commit and package SHA-256/npm integrity during the final CLI check; require unique hash-bound receipts for exact-runtime, browser/accessibility, security, package-contract, per-platform package, registry provenance and production verification.
defects.regression_test: The validator fixture rejects a dirty or mismatched authoritative candidate, missing final automation, untyped platform claims, absent registry or production proof and receipt reuse.

defects.summary: A live DuskDS public-node outage was classified as DuskEVM pre-launch planning and omitted from the common DuskDS recovery view.
defects.root_cause: DuskDS Access and Inspect reused the generic rpc-unavailable identifier, which the troubleshooting classifier reserved for the pre-launch DuskEVM path.
defects.fix: Add a dedicated duskds-public-node-unavailable blocker and active DuskDS troubleshooting record, use it for Access and Inspect failures, and include it in both common and all-reviewed DuskDS recovery views.
defects.regression_test: App verifies the dedicated DuskDS record is active and reachable in both common and all-reviewed scopes while the DuskEVM rpc-unavailable record remains Planning.

defects.summary: Automatic Local Actions preflight rejected an otherwise legitimate supported Windows result when a sanitized tool version string was between 129 and 160 characters.
defects.root_cause: The companion response sanitizer allowed 160 version characters while the consuming Studio schema allowed only 128, so the producer could emit data its own UI correctly refused.
defects.fix: Align the companion sanitizer with the 128-character UI response contract so legitimate long Windows tool output remains bounded and the whole preflight result stays consumable.
defects.regression_test: serverSecurity constructs a long-path preflight result across the producer/consumer boundary and requires every sanitized version to fit 128 characters and pass the Studio isPreflightResult contract; exact-candidate browser retest remains required.

defects.summary: The public-release browser test asserted same-document URLs before route effects settled, masking that unsupported DuskEVM access, build and inspect hashes correctly canonicalize to the single setup pre-launch surface.
defects.root_cause: gotoExact used an immediate page.url comparison after same-document navigation, and the route table incorrectly listed DuskEVM-only guide hashes as exact public routes instead of testing their intentional replace-style canonicalization.
defects.fix: Wait for stable exact URLs, restrict the exact-route table to real surfaces, and explicitly assert that access, build and inspect replace to setup with the truthful DuskEVM pre-launch title and heading.
defects.regression_test: The corrected release test passed 10 of 10 concurrent Mobile Safari WebKit repetitions after the old contract failed every stronger-wait repetition at the canonical setup URL.

defects.summary: The final validation fixture could still pass a wholly synthetic campaign, and one challenge-review receipt could impersonate success, failure-recovery and final-candidate proof for every product surface.
defects.root_cause: Trace claims were typed by surface and outcome but were not restricted by evidence class, record status or exact-candidate execution provenance, so internally consistent self-authored JSON remained sufficient.
defects.fix: Restrict every outcome to compatible receipt-bound execution classes and statuses, forbid challenge reviews from proving pilot outcomes, bind final-candidate claims to the exact candidate and policy bytes, and keep validator-only fixture provenance unreachable from the production API and CLI.
defects.regression_test: The focused validator suite rejects a synthetic but internally consistent campaign, challenge trace claims, wrong class/status/surface/outcome bindings and attempts to enable fixture provenance through the production API.

defects.summary: Final validation did not require the intended 1.0.2 candidate and did not inspect the selected tarball's package name, version, embedded manifest, commit or exact inventory.
defects.root_cause: The policy named only the 1.0.1 baseline, while authoritative final inspection computed bytes for an arbitrary archive without validating its package semantics.
defects.fix: Declare version 1.0.2 and tag v1.0.2 as the intended candidate, safely inspect bounded regular package/ tar entries and checksums, and require package.json, package manifest, commit, file inventory and computed digests to agree with the exact candidate.
defects.regression_test: Focused fixtures reject wrong version, tag and embedded identity plus unsafe or altered archive inventory; the inspector also verified the existing real 1.0.1 tarball's 29 manifest-bound files.

defects.summary: Campaign evidence timestamps and declared revalidation windows were not enforced, allowing stale, future or chronologically impossible evidence to remain structurally valid.
defects.root_cause: The validator checked timestamp syntax but not ordering, future time, concrete maximum age or identity-triggered rerun requirements.
defects.fix: Encode concrete evidence-class freshness limits, including a 15-hour DuskDS public-node window, reject future or reversed timestamps, and require final evidence after the current source, package and policy identities were fixed.
defects.regression_test: Focused fixtures reject stale, future, reversed and pre-candidate evidence and require receipt-bound fresh DuskDS public-node proof in production verification.

defects.summary: Exact-package assurance did not preserve automatic-preflight compatibility in its aggregate receipt and only exercised the browser producer-consumer path on Ubuntu, despite the original schema failure being observed on Windows.
defects.root_cause: The browser smoke emitted local_actions_preflight_verified, but workflow checks and the three-platform aggregate contract omitted that field while Windows and macOS ran only looser lifecycle checks.
defects.fix: Define automatic-preflight compatibility as an exact installed-package response accepted by the actual checked-out Studio consumer guard with nonempty bounded tool/version coverage, run the safe loopback probe in every supported platform job, bind the consumer-source SHA-256 and closed-port proof, and preserve it in the aggregate receipt.
defects.regression_test: Repository contracts, 13 npm test files with 142 tests, npm build and a local exact installed-tarball probe pass with 17 tool rows, 6 versioned rows, actual consumer-schema acceptance and both ports closed; native three-platform workflow execution remains required.

defects.summary: The native package workflow and final validator computed incompatible inventory digests for the same exact npm tarball.
defects.root_cause: CI hashed a path-only list containing all 30 archive files, while the validator hashed the 29 content-bound package-manifest records with path, byte count and SHA-256.
defects.fix: Use the validator's bounded semantic tarball inspector in the build, platform and pilot workflow lanes and propagate one content-bound inventory digest plus the 29-file manifest count throughout assurance.
defects.regression_test: The public-repository contract requires at least three workflow uses of inspectNpmTarballBytes and rejects the old path-only join; focused validation inspects the exact 1.0.2 tarball as 29 content-bound files with inventory b2144dc5490788af215c9011e65f5a733919ce04ac308e105ec6122a58ae3d77.

defects.summary: The aggregate native-CI receipt lacked the provenance envelope required for direct final-validator ingestion and could be replaced by local self-attestation.
defects.root_cause: The workflow emitted only aggregate fields and no exact evidence payload, GitHub run/ref/commit/workflow/job identity, immutable single-file artifact ID, URL or byte digest.
defects.fix: Upload a compact exact evidence payload with upload-artifact v7 archive=false, then emit a directly consumable receipt binding those exact bytes and digest to the immutable GitHub run, main ref, candidate commit, workflow, job, artifact ID and three native runner receipts.
defects.regression_test: Focused fixtures reject missing provenance, local-self-attested mode, wrong run commit, invalid artifact digest and any payload divergence; public workflow contracts require the single-file artifact and complete machine-observed envelope.

defects.summary: A failed exact-candidate execution recorded after a clear final challenge did not force another challenge review.
defects.root_cause: Challenge chronology considered only passing pilots, retests and automation, so a later failed exact-candidate observation could fall outside the ordering gate.
defects.fix: Require the clear final challenge to postdate every exact-candidate pilot, retest and automation record regardless of result, plus package, registry and production evidence.
defects.regression_test: An adversarial final fixture appends a failed exact-candidate pilot after the clear challenge and is rejected until the challenge is rerun.

defects.summary: The final tarball inspector accepted nonzero data appended after the two required TAR end blocks.
defects.root_cause: Parsing stopped after the complete end marker without verifying that all remaining decompressed bytes were zero padding.
defects.fix: Scan every byte after the end marker and reject the archive if any trailing byte is nonzero.
defects.regression_test: The focused tar fixture appends a nonzero 512-byte block after two zero end blocks and requires inspectNpmTarballBytes to reject it.

defects.summary: Package CI proved a clean checkout before restoration and build but did not recheck tracked or untracked source state after all package and browser operations.
defects.root_cause: The workflow's exact-commit claim relied on its initial checkout state even though restore, build, tests, packaging and browser smoke ran afterward.
defects.fix: Recheck HEAD, tracked changes, staged changes and all untracked files after restoration, build, tests, packaging and browser smoke and before uploading assurance evidence.
defects.regression_test: The public-repository contract requires the post-operation HEAD, git diff, cached diff and full untracked-status checks in the build-package job; native CI execution remains required.

defects.summary: The new GitHub package-assurance receipt recorded a bare tarball filename that the final validator would reject as a noncanonical candidate path.
defects.root_cause: The workflow reused its runner-local TARBALL basename for the durable evidence record while the final gate requires a safe repository-relative path under output/npm.
defects.fix: Emit the canonical output/npm/<exact candidate artifact> path in the evidence payload while retaining the separately bound candidate artifact name and package hashes.
defects.regression_test: The public-repository contract requires the exact output/npm/${EXPECTED_CANDIDATE_ARTIFACT} producer expression, while comprehensive final fixtures reject unsafe package paths.

defects.summary: The agent-pilot collector still used the obsolete path-only 30-file inventory and rejected the exact candidate's canonical 29-record content inventory.
defects.root_cause: The native workflow inventory producer was repaired without updating the collector's duplicated tarball inventory calculation or its synthetic fixture.
defects.fix: Derive the pilot digest and count from the exact manifest-bound path/byte/SHA-256 records returned by manifest validation, excluding package-manifest.json just like build, platform and final validation.
defects.regression_test: The exact Node collector fixture now uses the content-bound digest and asserts two manifest-bound files; the public contract rejects the obsolete newline-joined archive-path algorithm.

defects.summary: The final gate required evidence-ledger HEAD to equal the immutable package commit, creating an impossible post-CI evidence completion cycle.
defects.root_cause: Source/package identity and the later evidence-ledger identity were conflated in one clean-HEAD equality. The first repair separated them but checked only ancestor-or-equal, so the package commit itself still passed as its own purported descendant.
defects.fix: Keep the package bound to its immutable tag and manifest commit, require a different clean descendant evidence-ledger commit, and fail unless every change since the source tag is confined to the three explicit evidence/receipt/report paths.
defects.regression_test: Focused fixtures accept a strict clean descendant, explicitly reject evidence-ledger equality with the package commit, and reject release-source changes or unsafe ledger paths; public contracts require strict-descendant, ancestor and path-diff checks.

defects.summary: The final validator trusted locally supplied GitHub run and artifact metadata without independently querying GitHub or downloading the claimed evidence bytes.
defects.root_cause: Structural receipt checks were treated as provenance even though the same local campaign could fabricate both the receipt and its GitHub envelope.
defects.fix: At the production final CLI boundary, query the exact successful first-attempt main-branch run, validate workflow/ref/commit/artifact metadata, download the direct artifact, and compare its exact bytes and digest with the embedded payload before accepting assurance.
defects.regression_test: Focused final fixtures reject absent or divergent live verification, the GitHub downloader's adversarial API/download suite remains in the full test chain, and public contracts require the independent download path.

defects.summary: A clear final challenge could share the same timestamp as the latest failed exact-candidate observation and still pass chronology.
defects.root_cause: The ordering predicate rejected only challenge timestamps strictly earlier than the latest evidence instead of requiring the challenge to be strictly later.
defects.fix: Reject a clear challenge whose timestamp is earlier than or equal to any final-candidate pilot, retest, automation, package, registry or production observation.
defects.regression_test: A focused final fixture adds a failed exact-candidate execution ending at the exact challenge timestamp and now requires another challenge.

defects.summary: Pull-request, workflow-call and manual-dispatch assurance runs emitted receipts that the final validator correctly accepts only from a main-branch push.
defects.root_cause: The reusable aggregate job served build outputs and durable final receipt production without separating nonfinal event contexts from the release-evidence context.
defects.fix: Continue aggregate assurance for reusable callers, but upload the immutable final evidence payload and receipt only on a first-attempt push to refs/heads/main.
defects.regression_test: The public workflow contract requires the first-attempt main-push guard on all three final-evidence upload/finalization steps.

defects.summary: Aggregate package assurance marked cleanup passed from the policy list instead of deriving it from each platform's cleanup observation.
defects.root_cause: Platform receipts omitted install/cleanup fields and aggregate check_results were generated unconditionally for every required check.
defects.fix: Delete and verify bounded test roots on POSIX and Windows, remove the temporary Windows user profile and account, record install_smoke and cleanup_smoke per platform, and derive every aggregate check result and evidence reference from required receipt fields on all three runners.
defects.regression_test: Public workflow contracts require bounded root, user and profile cleanup, absent-path verification, per-platform install/cleanup fields and field-derived aggregate checks; the final validator independently re-derives check references from downloaded platform records.

defects.summary: A test-fixture-accepting comprehensive-validation function remained exported from the production module.
defects.root_cause: The production CLI correctly disabled fixture provenance, but unit-test convenience was still exposed as a public module API and made the broader security claim inaccurate.
defects.fix: Remove the fixture-accepting export and make focused final fixtures use production-shaped receipts through the normal production validator while retaining explicit fixture-rejection coverage.
defects.regression_test: The public repository contract forbids the exported fixture helper, and the focused suite proves validator-test-fixture provenance is still rejected by the production API.

defects.summary: The subsequent-version tag workflow could publish a freshly rebuilt tag-run package before proving it was byte-identical to the reviewed main-push candidate.
defects.root_cause: The tag workflow bound npm publication to its own reusable assurance outputs, but the main-push final receipt and reviewed local package came from separate builds; any cross-run difference would be detected only after immutable registry publication.
defects.fix: Before npm publication, query GitHub for the unique successful first-attempt main-push assurance run at the exact tag commit, validate its run and artifact metadata, download both main and tag candidates, require raw SHA-256, SHA-512 integrity, semantic inventory and every byte to match, and publish the main-reviewed bytes.
defects.regression_test: A focused adversarial test rejects different tag-build bytes and wrong main-artifact digests; the public workflow contract requires Actions-read scope, exact run/ref/event/attempt lookup, remote artifact download, cross-run binding and durable publication-receipt fields. Native GitHub API/action execution remains required.

defects.summary: Windows npm assurance removed its temporary local user fail-closed but did not explicitly assert that the account was absent afterward.
defects.root_cause: Post-cleanup verification covered bounded roots and the user profile but relied only on Remove-LocalUser returning success for account deletion.
defects.fix: After cleanup, query Get-LocalUser for the exact bounded test account and fail before writing the platform receipt if it still exists.
defects.regression_test: The public repository contract now requires the explicit local-account absence postcondition; Windows native CI remains required.

defects.summary: The retained formal Phase 5 gate still targeted 1.0.1 and synthetic obsolete receipt shapes that the current 1.0.2 assurance and publication workflows can never emit.
defects.root_cause: The package workflows evolved to expanded native receipts, a nested first-attempt main-push assurance envelope and cross-run publication fields while the separately retained Phase 5 policy, template, evaluator and fixtures remained on their earlier flat contracts.
defects.fix: Bump Phase 5 evidence to schema 10 and 1.0.2, validate the literal expanded platform, nested assurance and cross-run publication shapes, require main-push provenance, and independently download both the inner assurance payload and the exact reviewed package artifact.
defects.regression_test: Workflow-shaped positive fixtures pass; adversarial fixtures reject schema 9, 1.0.1, the old flat assurance receipt, workflow-dispatch provenance, platform and payload divergence, wrong inner artifacts and every cross-run publication field.

defects.summary: Publication resolved an immutable main-assurance artifact ID but downloaded the candidate by run-scoped name instead of consuming that exact ID.
defects.root_cause: The first cross-run binding repair recorded the selected artifact ID but passed only its unique name and run to download-artifact.
defects.fix: Emit artifact_id as a resolver output and pass it through download-artifact's immutable artifact-ids input while retaining the run, digest and byte checks.
defects.regression_test: Resolver and public workflow contracts require the artifact-ID output and exact ID download path.

defects.summary: The main-assurance resolver searched only the newest 100 successful runs, so a valid exact-commit run could become undiscoverable.
defects.root_cause: The resolver filtered the bounded first API page locally instead of using GitHub's exact head_sha query filter.
defects.fix: Add the exact encoded candidate commit as head_sha to the successful main-push workflow-run query and retain the unique first-attempt result check.
defects.regression_test: Focused resolver and public repository contracts require the exact head_sha query and still reject duplicate, rerun and wrong-commit results.

challenge_reviews.summary: High-assurance plan challenge required an independent campaign contract, evidence-class separation, exact quotas, clean-state proof, complete machine-surface inventory, safe release sequencing, stop rules and durable freshness-bound evidence.
